description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Request the cheapest slope stability quote option under $15,000 for a highway embankment
tasks.description: Start from the homepage, navigate to slope stability services, and submit a quote request for a highway embankment project in Denver with a construction start date of 06/15/2025. Among the displayed service levels, select the cheapest option that includes stability modeling and keep the total budget at or below $15,000 before submitting the form.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation menu, click on "Services" and then select "Slope Stability & Retaining Walls" from the dropdown.
On the slope stability service page, click the prominent "Get a Quote" or "Request a Quote" button.
In the "Service Type" dropdown on the quote form, select "Slope stability analysis for embankments".
In the "Project Type" field, open the dropdown and choose "Highway / Roadway".
In the location fields, type "Denver" in the city field and "80202" in the ZIP/Postal Code field.
In the "Anticipated Construction Start Date" field, enter "06/15/2025" using the date picker or by typing it in.
Scroll to the "Service Level" options, compare the listed prices, and click the radio button for the cheapest option that mentions "stability modeling" in its description.
In the budget section, type "15000" into the "Maximum Budget (USD)" field and then click the "Submit Quote Request" button at the bottom of the form.

tasks.name: Select a foundation investigation package with CPT and consolidation testing under $12,000 and save it to a plan
tasks.description: Find subsurface investigation services for building foundations, filter packages to stay under $12,000, choose a package that explicitly includes both CPT soundings and consolidation testing, and save that package under a specific project name.
steps: From the homepage, click on "Services" in the top navigation and choose "Subsurface Investigation" from the list.
On the subsurface investigation page, click the "Packages & Pricing" tab or section to view available investigation packages.
Use the "Project Type" filter or dropdown and select "Building foundations" to narrow the package list.
Locate the price filter or slider and set the maximum price to "12000" so only packages with prices at or below $12,000 are shown.
Scroll through the filtered package cards and identify the first package whose features list includes both "CPT soundings" and "Consolidation (oedometer) testing".
Click the title or "View Details" button on that package to open its detailed package page.
On the package detail page, click the "Select this package" or equivalent selection button.
When the "Save to project" or "Save plan" dialog appears, type "Headquarters foundation study" in the project name field.
Click the "Save to My Plans" or "Save" button to store this selected package under that project name.

tasks.name: Find a pile foundation case study for a commercial building over $5M and open its details
tasks.description: Navigate to the project portfolio, filter to commercial building projects that use pile foundations, then within the filtered results choose the first project with a listed value greater than $5M and open its case study details page.
steps: On the homepage, click on "Projects" or "Case Studies" in the main navigation menu.
On the projects page, click the "Filters" button or panel and in the "Sector" dropdown select "Commercial buildings".
In the same filter area, open the "Foundation type" filter and check or select the option for "Pile foundations".
Apply the filters by clicking the "Apply" or "Update Results" button so only commercial projects with pile foundations are displayed.
Locate the "Project Value" filter or sorting option and, if available, set the minimum value to "$5M" or select a range that starts at "$5M".
If a sort dropdown is present, choose "Project value – High to Low" to sort the filtered results by value.
In the resulting project cards, identify the first card whose "Project value" label shows more than "$5M" and click the project title on that card.
On the opened project page, scroll down until the main case study content is visible and click the "Read full case study" or similar link to ensure the detailed case study section is open.

tasks.name: Download the latest intermediate retaining wall design checklist published after 2022
tasks.description: Access the resources library, filter for intermediate-level guides or checklists on retaining wall design published after January 1, 2022, sort by newest first, and download the most recent retaining wall design checklist as a PDF.
steps: From any page, scroll to the top navigation and click on "Resources" or "Knowledge Center".
On the resources page, open the "Resource Type" dropdown and select "Guides & Checklists".
In the "Topic" or "Discipline" filter, choose "Retaining walls" or the closest matching retaining wall design topic.
In the "Level" filter, select "Intermediate" to limit the resources to that experience level.
Find the "Published Date" filter or range selector and set the start date to "01/01/2022" so only items published on or after that date are included.
Use the sort dropdown to select "Newest first" or "Most recent" as the sorting method.
In the resulting list, locate the first item whose title includes "Retaining wall design checklist" or similar wording and click its title to open the resource detail page.
On the resource detail page, click the "Download PDF" button or link to initiate the download of the checklist.

tasks.name: Book a 30-minute virtual consultation with a foundation design specialist on a specific date and time
tasks.description: Use the consultation booking tool to schedule a 30-minute virtual meeting with a foundation design specialist for a concept-stage project, selecting a specific date and a time slot between 10:00 and 11:00 AM, and complete the booking form with your contact information.
steps: Go to the homepage and click on the "Book a Consultation" or "Schedule a Call" button in the header or hero section.
On the consultation page, under "Meeting Type", select the option labeled "Virtual meeting".
In the "Specialty" or "Consultant expertise" dropdown, choose "Foundation design".
Use the date picker to select a specific date (for example, choose "03/18/2025" or the next available Tuesday shown on the calendar).
In the list of available time slots for that date, select a 30-minute time slot that starts between 10:00 AM and 10:30 AM (for example, click on "10:00–10:30 AM").
In the project stage field or dropdown, select "Concept / Early design" as the project stage.
Fill in the name field with "Alex Martinez" and the email field with "alex.martinez@example.com" in the booking form.
Scroll to the bottom of the form and click the "Confirm Booking" or "Schedule Consultation" button to finalize the appointment.

tasks.name: Use the bearing capacity calculator and save the result to a named project
tasks.description: Open the geotechnical calculator tool, calculate the ultimate bearing capacity for a 2.0 m wide footing in medium dense sand at 1.5 m depth with a factor of safety of 3, and then save the calculation result under a new project named 'Office Tower A'.
steps: From the main navigation, hover over or click "Tools" and select "Bearing Capacity Calculator" from the dropdown list.
On the calculator page, open the "Soil type" dropdown and select "Medium dense sand".
In the footing width field, type "2.0" and ensure the units selector next to it is set to "m" (meters).
In the footing depth or embedment depth field, type "1.5" and leave the units as "m" (meters).
Locate the "Factor of Safety" input field and enter the value "3".
If there is a "Project location" field, type "Chicago" into it to associate the calculation with that city.
Click the "Calculate" or "Run Calculation" button to generate the bearing capacity result.
After the result is displayed, click the "Save Result" or "Save to Project" button next to the output.
In the dialog that appears, type "Office Tower A" in the project name field and click the "Save" button to store the calculation under that project.

tasks.name: Subscribe to the newsletter with specific geotechnical topics and monthly frequency
tasks.description: Use the on-site subscription form to sign up for the firm's newsletter using a specified email address, selecting slope stability and deep foundations as topics of interest and choosing a monthly email frequency.
steps: Scroll to the bottom of any page until the website footer is visible.
In the footer, locate the newsletter subscription form and click into the email input field.
Type "client.geotech@example.com" into the email field.
In the "Topics of interest" checklist or dropdown, select the options for "Slope stability" and "Deep foundations".
If an option for "Ground improvement" is present, leave it unselected so only the two chosen topics remain selected.
In the "Email frequency" dropdown, choose "Monthly".
If there is a checkbox for "I am a consulting engineer" or "Professional role", select "Consulting engineer" from the options.
Click the "Subscribe", "Sign Up", or similar button to complete the newsletter subscription.

tasks.name: Build a shortlist of three senior geotechnical experts for a West region slope stability project
tasks.description: Navigate to the team directory, filter for West region staff with geotechnical expertise, then open profiles and add three experts to a project team shortlist. Each selected expert must show at least 10 years of experience and list slope stability or landslide analysis as a key skill.
steps: From the homepage, click on "About" in the main navigation and then select "Our Team" from the dropdown.
On the team page, open the "Region" filter and choose "West" or "Western US" as the location.
In the "Discipline" or "Expertise" filter, select "Geotechnical engineering".
Apply additional keyword or skill filters by typing "slope stability" into any available search or "Skills" filter field.
From the filtered list of team members, click on the first profile that appears to open that person's detailed bio page.

On the profile page, scan the experience summary and, if the profile lists "10+ years" or more experience and mentions "slope stability" or "landslide analysis" as a skill, click the "Add to project team" or "Shortlist" button for that expert.

Use the back navigation or team directory link to return to the filtered team list and repeat the process of opening profiles until you have added a total of three qualifying experts to the project team shortlist.
After adding the third expert, click on the "View project team" or "Shortlist" link in the header or sidebar to open the page that lists the three selected team members.
